ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Kirlia,' National Pokedex no. 281, small humanoid white-dress figure with green hair and red horns, dancing in a field — matches Kirlia canonical design exactly. PokemonTCG.io confirms card name directly.

trivia_bullets.text: Kirlia is National Pokedex no. 281, a Stage 1 Psychic/Fairy type introduced in Generation III (Hoenn). It evolves from Ralts (level 20) and into either Gardevoir (level 30) or — if male — Gallade (via Dawn Stone, added in Generation IV). Burning Shadows (SM3) released August 5, 2017. [PokemonTCG.io: sm3-92; WebSearch: Bulbapedia snippet]

Kirlia was Psychic-only in Generations III-V; the Fairy type was added in Generation VI (X/Y, 2013) when the Fairy type was introduced to the franchise. This card prints it as a Fairy-type in the TCG, reflecting the post-Gen VI designation. [WebSearch: Bulbapedia snippet; PokemonTCG.io: sm3-92 types]

The Ralts line's design is said to evoke meadow elves — beings from folklore believed to possess magical powers and supernatural beauty, capable of either helping or hindering ordinary people. Kirlia's dancing behavior is canon: it spins and dances when happy, powered by the positive emotions of its Trainer. [WebSearch: Bulbapedia snippet; PokemonTCG.io: sm3-92 flavor text]
'The cheerful spirit of its Trainer gives it energy for its psychokinetic power. It spins and dances when happy.' [PokemonTCG.io: sm3-92]

related_cards.name: Ralts
related_cards.note: First stage of the line; evolves into Kirlia at level 20

related_cards.name: Gardevoir
related_cards.note: Final evolution (any gender); one of the most-printed Pokemon in the TCG

related_cards.name: Gallade
related_cards.note: Male-only alternate final evolution via Dawn Stone; Psychic/Fighting type
